mission.title: Install and Configure Etcd on Kubernetes
mission.description: etcd is a distributed reliable key-value store designed for critical data in distributed systems. Installing etcd allows you to leverage its high availability, security, and performance for your applications.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official etcd Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add etcd https://charts.etcd.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install etcd using Helm
steps.description:
Install etcd with a specified version and create a namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install etcd etcd/etcd --version 3.6.8 --namespace etcd --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the etcd pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace etcd
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the etcd deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment etcd --namespace etcd --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"512Mi"},"requests":{"cpu":"250m","memory":"256Mi"}}}]'
```

steps.title: Set up TLS for secure communication
steps.description:
To enable TLS for etcd, create a secret with your certificates and update the deployment accordingly.
```bash
kubectl create secret tls etcd-tls --cert=path/to/etcd.crt --key=path/to/etcd.key --namespace etcd
kubectl patch deployment etcd --namespace etcd --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/args/-", "value": "--cert-file=/etc/ssl/etcd/etcd.crt"},{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/args/-", "value": "--key-file=/etc/ssl/etcd/etcd.key"}]'
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the etcd release from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall etcd --namespace etcd
```

uninstall.title: Delete the etcd namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the namespace created for etcd to clean up resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace etcd
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs if necessary
uninstall.description:
If any Custom Resource Definitions were created, delete them as well.
```bash
kubectl delete crd etcds.etcd.io
```

upgrade.title: Backup current etcd data
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current etcd data to avoid data loss.
```bash
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot save snapshot.db --endpoints=https://<etcd-endpoint>:2379 --cert=path/to/etcd.crt --key=path/to/etcd.key --cacert=path/to/ca.crt
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the etcd Helm chart.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade etcd to the latest version
upgrade.description:
Perform the upgrade to the specified version.
```bash
helm upgrade etcd etcd/etcd --version 3.6.8 --namespace etcd
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the etcd pods are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace etcd
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your etcd pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace etcd
``` 
Ensure that there are enough resources (CPU/memory) available in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: etcd not starting due to configuration errors
troubleshooting.description:
Check the logs of the etcd pod for any configuration-related errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace etcd
``` 
Correct any misconfigurations in the Helm values or deployment settings.

troubleshooting.title: TLS connection issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter TLS errors, verify that the certificates are correctly mounted and valid.
```bash
kubectl describe secret etcd-tls --namespace etcd
``` 
Ensure that the paths to the certificates in the deployment match the mounted secrets.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of etcd will have the pods running in the 'Running' state and responding to requests. You can verify this by checking the pod status and ensuring that the etcd service is accessible.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add etcd https://charts.etcd.io
helm repo update

kubectl create secret tls etcd-tls --cert=path/to/etcd.crt --key=path/to/etcd.key --namespace etcd
kubectl patch deployment etcd --namespace etcd --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/args/-", "value": "--cert-file=/etc/ssl/etcd/etcd.crt"},{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/args/-", "value": "--key-file=/etc/ssl/etcd/etcd.key"}]'

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed.
